rulings.Case Title: MISSION HOUSING DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION VS. MATTIE LEESON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendant Mattie Leeson'S Response To Plaintiff'S Notice Of Deposition And Request For Sanctions Against Defendant In Amount Of $795.00
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 18, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFF MISSION HOUSING DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT MATTIE LEESON'S RESPONSE TO PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF DEPOSITION AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT IN AMOUNT OF $795.00 is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Defendant shall appear for deposition on a mutually agreed upon date no later than April 5, 2024. If parties cannot agree on a date, deposition shall proceed on March 29, 2024. Plaintiff to send a 5-day notice. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ALI A. AMINI ET AL VS. ANDREW LINTON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 18, 2024 line 2. DEFENDANT ANDREW LINTON, ANGELINA GREEP MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Hearing Required to address the following: "Defendants Andrew Linton and Angelina Meilani Greep are entitled to summary judgment because Plaintiff waived the right to evict Defendant for nuisance. Plaintiff accepted Defendant's rent checks after the date the notice to quit expired." (Notice of Motion 2:7-10) and applicability of the foregoing to the facts of this case. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
